name: Chocolate Mint Snow-Top Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine flour, baking powder, and salt; set aside.
2. In a small saucepan over low heat, melt 1 cup mint chocolate chips. (Alternatively, microwave the chips in a bowl on HIGH power for 1 minute; stir. Then microwave on HIGH power for an additional 30 seconds longer; stir until smooth.)
3. In a large mixing bowl, beat butter and sugar until creamy.
4. Add melted chips and vanilla extract to the mixture. Beat until combined.
5. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
6. Gradually add the flour mixture to the butter mixture, stirring gently until just incorporated.
7. Stir in the remaining 1/2 cup mint chocolate chips.
8. Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes until firm.
9. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
10. Shape the dough into 1-inch balls and coat each with confectioners sugar.
11. Place the cookie balls on ungreased baking sheets.
12. Bake for 10-12 minutes, or until the tops appear cracked.
13. Allow the cookies to cool for 2 minutes before removing them from the sheets and placing them on a wire rack to cool completely. Makes about 3 dozen cookies.
